response.justification: While the candidate is a capable full-stack engineer with strong software craftsmanship practices, they fundamentally lack the required expertise for this role. The position demands 8 years of C++ programming; this candidate has minimal recent C++ experience (6 months at National Instruments in 2014-2015, none since). Similarly, the job requires 5 years building large-scale infrastructure or distributed systems; the candidate's background is primarily web and product engineering. For a mid-senior technical lead of a cloud compute platform, these gaps are disqualifying.
strengths: Demonstrated ability to establish engineering best practices including TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and peer mentoring through workshops and code katas
Proven capacity to build and maintain large technical projects as evidenced by founding GISCollective and multiple open-source libraries
Extensive full-stack development experience across multiple languages and platforms showing adaptability and technical versatility
concerns: Minimal C++ experience: only ~6 months a decade ago with no recent work in the language; the role requires 8 years of C++ as a core requirement for cloud compute platform engineering

Limited infrastructure and distributed systems experience: background is primarily web and product engineering; mentions of microservices and performance optimization do not substitute for 5 years of dedicated systems-level infrastructure work
No evidence of formal technical leadership role: has conducted workshops and organized code katas but no demonstrated experience mentoring engineers or leading technical strategy within organizations
